Name: "I need the list to look like a clean, well-organized spreadsheet. No hidden columns.”
"No downloading resumes. I need to see it side-by-side with my notes."
"If I select a checkbox, give me my bulk actions instantly—don't make me hunt for a menu.”
"Keep the action buttons sticky; I shouldn't have to scroll to reject or advance someone.”
"If they applied before, I need to know immediately before I even open their profile.”
About Layout: "How can I set up my screen so everything I need is visible without scrolling or opening new browser tabs?"
About Context: "I need to know the full story of this candidate—their resume, their GitHub, and their history with our company—all at a single glance."
About Efficiency: "The UI should adapt to my actions. It should get out of my way when I'm reading, but be right there when I'm ready to act."
About Hierarchy: "The most critical data (Location, Right-to-Work, Current Title) needs to be prioritized visually over secondary data."
Scans horizontal rows looking for specific data points (Badges for "Past Applicant", Location, Title).
Relies on persistent left-hand filters to instantly manipulate the main data table without page reloads.
Selects multiple checkboxes to trigger the appearance of a floating/sticky bulk-action bar.
Navigates a split-screen view: reads the native PDF on the left while simultaneously typing in the right-hand workspace.
Toggles between 'Scorecard', 'Team Notes', and 'History' tabs in the workspace panel without losing sight of the candidate's resume.
Empowered by an interface that responds intelligently to their workflow (like dynamic bulk-action bars and sticky headers).
Relieved when all necessary candidate context (social links, historical data, resume) is centralized on one unified screen.
Frustrated by hidden information (e.g., critical data trapped behind "View More" toggles or deep dropdown menus).
Confident in making quick hiring decisions because the UI prioritizes scannability and keeps the "Advance" and "Reject" buttons constantly accessible.
